title: Multi-stage builds separate build-time from runtime
description: A multi-stage Dockerfile compiles in a fat builder stage and copies only the produced artifacts into a lean runtime stage, so toolchains and secrets never reach the shipped image.
body: Use when reasoning about why an image is large or leaks build secrets. In a multi-stage build, the first stage holds compilers, dev dependencies, and build credentials; the final stage starts from a minimal base and uses COPY --from to take only the runtime artifacts, so build tooling and any secrets used during build are left behind in a discarded stage. Do not assume a secret used only in the builder stays out of the image if you collapse stages. Actions: keep build and runtime stages separate and copy only artifacts forward. Validation signals include the final image lacking compilers and a layer scan showing no build secrets. Risk boundary: a secret passed via ARG can still persist in intermediate layers; treat build-time secrets as requiring BuildKit secret mounts, not ARG.
